rationale: Title Y matches JACC's concise, readable style and reads with immediate clarity, while Title X is over-long and cluttered with redundant qualifiers despite its greater specificity.
loser_weakness: Title X packs too many noun-pile qualifiers ('orthotopic,' 'transvalvular CIED leads,' 'multinational') into an already long pre-colon clause, hurting readability and journal fit without a proportionate gain in essential information.
